commit.message:
feat(system): Task Manager, ILM, and Templates troubleshooting pages (#1938)

Adds three read-only troubleshooting pages under System nav for
high-friction operational workflows that previously required raw API
calls.

### New pages

- **Task Manager** (`/cluster-tasks`) — KPI cards (running, cancellable,
long-running >60s, oldest), searchable/sortable table by
action/node/runtime, detail flyover with raw JSON
- **Index Lifecycle Management** (`/ilm`) — KPI cards (managed indices,
error count, policy count, phase distribution), dual-tab
indices/policies tables with error-first default sort, detail flyover
surfacing `failed_step` + `step_info.reason`
- **Index Templates** (`/templates`) — KPI cards (index/component
counts, data-stream enabled, high-priority ≥500), dual-tab
index/component template tables, detail flyover showing composition
order and simulated output

### Implementation

- **Types**: `taskTypes.ts`, `ilmTypes.ts`, `templateTypes.ts` —
flattened row types for table rendering alongside raw API response
shapes
- **Client methods**: `listTasks`, `cancelTask`, `getIlmPolicies`,
`getIlmExplain`, `getIndexTemplates`, `getComponentTemplates`,
`simulateIndexTemplate`
- **Hooks**: `useTasks`, `useIlm`, `useTemplates`,
`useSimulatedIndexTemplate` — standard `useQuery` + `DataFetchResult`
pattern; `useIlm` and `useTemplates` use `Promise.all` for parallel
fetches
- **Manifest**: Tasks promoted from hidden to sidebar-visible; ILM
(order 31) and Templates (order 22) added under System
- **Ingest Pipelines**: pipeline detail panel now includes expandable
processor hotspots with matched processor config details
- **Mocks**: Full mock data + route handlers in
`elasticsearch-mocks.mjs` for all new endpoints
- **Tests**: 39 unit tests (sorting, filtering, metric derivation), E2E
smoke test for route/render, AppSidebar test updated

All pages follow existing conventions: `LinearProgress` (not
`CircularProgress`), `COMPONENT_HEIGHTS` tokens, right-side `Drawer`
flyover, `EmptyState` for zero results, `PageHeader` +
`OverviewInfoCard` layout.

commit.message:
fix: correct three mobile navbar regressions in App.css

1. Add missing --text-secondary CSS variable to :root
   - mobile-nav-section-title and other elements used var(--text-secondary)
     which was undefined; text rendered invisible/empty on mobile drawer

2. Move mobile drawer CSS outside @media (max-width: 768px) block
   - .mobile-drawer, .mobile-drawer-overlay, and all related classes were
     scoped to the mobile media query; on desktop (>=769px) these classes
     had no styles, so the drawer elements rendered as unstyled blocks in
     the document flow (visible layout corruption on desktop)
   - Base styles (position:fixed, transform:translateX(-100%), z-index) must
     always apply so the drawer stays off-screen until JS opens it

3. Add !important to .search-page-container aside { display: none }
   - The <aside> has an inline style prop with display:'flex'; CSS class
     selectors cannot override inline styles without !important
   - Without this, the 280px sidebar never hides on mobile viewports,
     leaving only ~95px for search columns on a 375px screen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add --insecure and --open-dashboard flags

Add two opt-in security relaxation flags for the dashboard:

--insecure: Sets tunnel to anonymous access so no Azure AD login
is required to reach the tunnel URL. Warns at CLI and in server logs.

--open-dashboard: Disables the dashboard passcode (?key= parameter)
requirement for remote access. Warns loudly at both CLI and startup.

Both flags can also be set via config (tunnel_access: "anonymous",
require_key: false) for persistent configuration. Both default to
the secure behavior — users must explicitly opt in.

Environment variables OTTO_TUNNEL_ACCESS and OTTO_OPEN_DASHBOARD
propagate these settings through the daemon fork.

commit.message:
fix(llm): use native tool_calls format for MiniMax M2.5

M2.5 fully supports OpenAI-compatible tool_calls/tool role messages.
The previous workaround (converting to plain text '[Appel outils: name]')
was designed for M2.1 and actively caused hallucinations in M2.5:
agents couldn't see their prior tool call arguments in history, so
they fabricated what they 'had done' → HALLUCINATION/ECHO/XXX escalations.

Changes:
- History mangling (tool→user, assistant+tool_calls→text) scoped to M2.1 only
- tool_choice: 'required' for M2.5 (was 'auto', silently ignored)
- parallel_tool_calls: false for M2.5 (sequential tool calls are more reliable)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix BLE scan: seed cache from bluetoothctl devices before live scan

QBluetoothDeviceDiscoveryAgent only finds actively-advertising devices.
On Linux, pre-populate the cache from 'bluetoothctl devices' (which lists
every device BlueZ has ever seen, including bonded/paired ones not currently
advertising). The live 5-second scan then updates names and finds any new
advertisers. The emitted list is the union of both sources.

On scan error, cached devices are still returned so the user can connect
to a known device even if the adapter failed to scan.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: switch benchmark to collective warmup across all test cases (#148)

Split the bench() method into separate warmup() and measure() functions.
Run warmup for ALL SVG test cases across ALL Java engines before any
measurement begins. This ensures the JIT C2 compiler profiles all code
paths (e.g., every branch in Surface::draw's tag switch) and avoids
deoptimization/recompilation during the measurement phase.

Previously, each test case warmed up independently, causing Surface::draw
to deoptimize 7 times as each new SVG element type triggered uncommon
traps. Only the last ~3 test cases measured with fully C2-optimized code.

Closes #147

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
